description: The topic doc and rubric expect a lowercase 'active' pill label; the rendered pill text is uppercase 'ACTIVE' (via CSS text-transform, markup itself is lowercase 'active'). Not wrong, just a documentation/implementation casing mismatch worth reconciling.
expected: Consistent casing between documented copy and rendered UI (either both lowercase or docs updated to reflect uppercase display).
actual: Rendered pill displays 'ACTIVE' in all caps; underlying text node is 'active'.
suggested_fix: No code fix needed (cosmetic small-caps styling is fine) — just note it if docs are audited.

description: The only real session available to click for a first-load walk was a very data-rich session (15 workflows, 3 subagents, $253, spanning 28h) — this is a poor 'first timer' representative session (the John persona in the story expects plain conversations only). Not a bug, but the available seed data doesn't match the 'first load, no workflows' scenario the story describes, so full comprehension-as-a-newcomer could not be verified against a simple case.
expected: A plain zero-workflow session available to fully exercise the John/first-timer path as documented.
actual: All non-empty sessions in the active project have workflows/subagents attached; the Active Session view is dense (multiple stat cards, Pareto bars, savings panel) on first click for every available session.
suggested_fix: Not a product bug — future walker runs should pick a project/session with zero workflows to fully verify the 'plain session' first-load path.
